RE: Campaign Statements For Unopposed Judges
To Whom It May Concern:
My wife is a Superior Court judge for Contra Costa County. She
recently received a "1989, Form 470, Candidate and Offlceholder
Campaign Statement -- Short Formrr instruction sheet as she is up
for election in June 1990. The instruction sheet says:
Note: Judges who will not be lj.sted on the ballot during a
calendar year, who have not received any contributj-ons, and
whose only expenditures for the year are contributions
aggregating l-ess than $1,000 which were made out of personal
funds are not required to file campaign statements. (emphasis
added )
My research, including review of Government Code section 84205,
FPPC regulation 18406, and pages L7 and l-8, section D, 1988
Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the
Politica1 Reform Act, indicates that even unopposed judges who have
no controlled committee, receive no campaign funds and whose only
expense is a filing fee paid out of personal funds stlll 
must file
at Least the Form 470. In fact, the footnote on page 18 of the
above referenced Information Manual states:
Judges who are unopposed and whose only expenditure is the
pal'ment of personal funds for the filing fee or statement
of qual-ifications must f j.1e a campaign statement.
Since the f ilj-ng deadline is March 22, L990, I would like to know
as soon as possible trhether your instruction sheet is in error or
has the Law changed and my research outdated? A1so, since I
enforce the provisions of the Political Reform Act for Contra Costa
County, I need to know the current and eorrect requirement for
filing of campaign statements.


Your prompt
caf l- me at
lvriting.
response wouLd
(4t51 646-4527
beif appreciated. If you desire, you may

Very truly yours ,
-Tv

. 
THE PERIOD COVERED 15 ALWAYS THE CALENDAR YEAR (JANUARY I THROUGH DECEMBER 31).
TO OETERMINE IF $I,OOO HAs BEEN RAIsED OR SPENT OR WILL BE RAISED OR sPENT, THE
CANDIDATE's PERsONAL TUND5 FOR THE FILING FEE OR sTATEMENT OF QUALIFICATIONS ARE
EXCLUDED. HOWEVER, SUCH PAYMENTs ARE "EXPENDITUREs" AND ARE REPORTABLE IF THE
CANDIDATE 15 SUEsEQUENTLY REQUIRED TO FILE A CAMPAIGN sTATEMENT LONG FORM (FORM
490). CONTRIBUTIONS MADE TO OTHER CANDIDATES, OFFICEHOLDERS AND COMMITTEES FROM A
CANDIDATE's OR OFFICEHOLDER's PERSONAL FUNDs OR THE PERSONAL FUNDS OF HIS OR HER SPOUsE
ARE INCLUDED FOR PURPOSES OF THE 
'I,OOO 
THRESHOLD.
NOTE: JUDGES WHO WILL NOT BE LISTED ON THE BALLOT DURING A CALENDAR YEAR, WHO HAVE NoT
RECEIVED ANY CONTRIBUTIONS, AND WHOSE ONLY EXPENDITUREs FOR THE YEAR ARE CONTRIBUTIONS
AGGREGATING LE55 THAN SI,OOO WHICH WERE MADE OUT OF PERSONAL FUNDS ARE NOT REQUIRED TO
FILE CAMPAIGN 5TATEMENT5.
IF THE FORM 470 15 FILED BY A CANDIDATE OR OFFICEHOLDER WITH A DECLAMTION OF
CANDIOACY, OR ON OR BEFORE THE FILING DEADLINE FOR THE FIRsT CAMPAIGN STATEMENT
REQUIRED FOR THE CURRENT CALENDAR YEAR, NO AODITIONAL CAMPAIGN STATEMENTs NEED BE
FILED FOR THAT CALENDAR YEAR 50 LONG A5 TOTAL RECEIPTS REMAIN LESS THAN $1,OOO AND
TOTAL EXPENDITURES REMAIN LE55 THAN $1,OOO. (IN MOsT CASES, THE FILING DEADLINE FOR THE
FIRST CAMPAIGN STATEMENT REQUIRED TO BE IITED BY OFFICIHOLDERs AND CANDIDATES NOT BEING
VOTED UPON IS JULY 3I. sEE THE "INFORMATION MANUAL ON CAMPAIGN DISCLOSURE PROVISIONS OF
THE POLITICAL REFORM ACT" FOR FURTHER INFORMAIION.)
(
IMPORTANT
,,COMMITTEES' MUST FILE SEMI-ANNUAL CAMPAIGN DISCLOSURE sTATEMENTS FOR EACH
SIX.MONTH PERIOD WHETHER OR NOT THEY HAD ANY ACTIVITY OURING THE sIX-MONTH
PERIOD COVERED BY THE STATEMENT.
OFFICEHOLDERs AND CANDIDATEs WHO HAVE RAISED OR sPENT $I,OOO OR MORE OR WHO
ANTICIPATE RAISING OR SPENDING 
'I,OOO 
OR MORE MUST FILE THE FORM 490, CANOIDATE
AND OFFICEHOLDER CAMPAIGN STATEMENT.LONG FORM.
OFFICEHOLDERs WHOSE SALARY 15 LE55 THAN SIOO PER MONTH AND JUDGES WHO HAVE A
CONTROLLED COMMITTEE, SHOULD REFER TO THE "INFORMATION MANUAL ON CAMPAIGN
DISCLOsURE PROVISIONs OF THE POTITICAL REFORM ACT' TO DETERMINE THEIR FILING
OBLIGATIONS.

sections.question: Since the filing deadline is March 22, 1990, I would like to know as soon as possible whether your instruction sheet is in error or has the Law changed and my research outdated? Also, since I enforce the provisions of the Political Reform Act for Contra Costa County, I need to know the current and correct requirement for filing of campaign statements.
sections.question_synthetic: The District Attorney of Contra Costa County seeks clarification on whether an unopposed Superior Court judge with no campaign committee, no contributions, and only a personal funds filing fee payment must file a Form 470 campaign statement, noting an apparent contradiction between the Form 470 instruction sheet and the FPPC Information Manual.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The document includes the Form 470 instruction sheet as a reference document. No FPPC conclusion or response is present. The letter requests urgent guidance due to a March 22, 1990 filing deadline.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Since the filing deadline is March 22, 1990, I would like to know as soon as possible whether your instruction sheet is in error or has the Law changed and my research outdated? Also, since I enforce the provisions of the Political Reform Act for Contra Costa County, I need to know the current and correct requirement for filing of campaign statements.

embedding.summary: The District Attorney of Contra Costa County requests urgent clarification from the FPPC regarding campaign statement filing requirements for unopposed judges, noting a discrepancy between the Form 470 instruction sheet (which appears to exempt such judges) and the FPPC Information Manual (which requires filing).
